description_long: This is a compact submachine gun with a utilitarian, industrial design, featuring a cylindrical metal receiver and a prominent detachable box magazine extending downward. The weapon has a simple, straight wooden pistol grip, which contrasts with the otherwise all-metal construction. The barrel is short and ends with a basic cylindrical muzzle device, while the upper receiver is smooth with minimal external features except for a charging handle and ejection port. The iron sights are fixed and integrated into the receiver and barrel assembly, providing basic aiming capability. The finish is matte and slightly worn, suggesting frequent handling or use. The overall appearance is rugged and functional, suitable for close-quarters urban environments.
description: A compact, rugged submachine gun with a metal body, wooden pistol grip, and detachable box magazine. It features a short barrel, fixed iron sights, and a utilitarian matte finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile, including the wooden pistol grip, detachable box magazine, and short barrel with a simple muzzle device.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular muzzle, front sight post, and the narrow profile of the receiver and magazine.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the ejection port, charging handle, and the overall symmetry of the weapon.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the receiver, the top of the wooden grip, and the alignment of the magazine.
